Despite their importance, the utility of these kernel methods was limited in large-scale learning settings due to their (super-)quadratic runtime and memory complexities. Moreover, most prior works on neural kernels have focused on the ReLU activation, mainly due to its popularity but also due to the difficulty of computing such kernels for general activations. In this work, we overcome such difficulties by providing methods to work with general activations. First, we compile and expand the list of activation functions admitting exact dual activation expressions to compute neural kernels. When the exact computation is unknown, we present methods to effectively approximate them. We propose a fast sketching method that approximates any multi-layered Neural Network Gaussian Process (NNGP) kernel and Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK) matrices for a wide range of activation functions, going beyond the commonly analyzed ReLU activation. This is done by showing how to approximate the neural kernels using the truncated Hermite expansion of any desired activation functions. While most prior works require data points on the unit sphere, our methods do not suffer from such limitations and are applicable to any dataset of points in $\mathbb{R}^d$. Furthermore, we provide a subspace embedding for NNGP and NTK matrices with near input-sparsity runtime and near-optimal target dimension which applies to any \emph{homogeneous} dual activation functions with rapidly convergent Taylor expansion. Empirically, with respect to exact convolutional NTK (CNTK) computation, our method achieves $106\times$ speedup for approximate CNTK of a 5-layer Myrtle network on CIFAR-10 dataset.
abstract_zh: 无限宽度限制通过在神经网络和核方法之间建立联系，阐明了深度学习的泛化和优化方面。尽管它们很重要，但由于它们的（超）二次运行时间和内存复杂性，这些内核方法的实用性在大规模学习环境中受到限制。此外，大多数先前关于神经内核的工作都集中在 ReLU 激活上，这主要是因为它的流行，但也因为计算此类内核用于一般激活的困难。在这项工作中，我们通过提供处理一般激活的方法来克服这些困难。首先，我们编译并扩展激活函数列表，允许精确的双激活表达式来计算神经内核。当确切的计算未知时，我们提出了有效近似它们的方法。我们提出了一种快速草图方法，可以近似任何多层神经网络高斯过程 (NNGP) 内核和神经正切内核 (NTK) 矩阵，以实现各种激活函数，超越了通常分析的 ReLU 激活。这是通过展示如何使用任何所需激活函​​数的截断埃尔米特展开来近似神经核来完成的。虽然大多数先前的工作需要单位球体上的数据点，但我们的方法不受此类限制，并且适用于 $\mathbb{R}^d$ 中的任何点数据集。此外，我们为 NNGP 和 NTK 矩阵提供了一个具有接近输入稀疏运行时间和接近最优目标维度的子空间嵌入，适用于任何具有快速收敛泰勒展开的 \emph{齐次} 对偶激活函数。根据经验，对于精确卷积 NTK (CNTK) 计算，我们的方法在 CIFAR-10 数据集上对 5 层 Myrtle 网络的近似 CNTK 实现了 106 倍加速。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种高效计算神经网络核嵌入的方法，适用于通用激活函数，解决了传统方法在大规模学习中的计算复杂度高和激活函数限制的问题。
AI.motivation: 尽管无限宽度神经网络与核方法的联系为深度学习提供了理论支持，但现有核方法因计算复杂度高且主要依赖ReLU激活函数，难以应用于大规模学习场景。
AI.method: 通过扩展支持精确对偶激活表达式的激活函数列表，结合截断Hermite展开近似任意激活函数的核，并提出适用于任意输入数据的子空间嵌入方法，降低计算复杂度。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10数据集上，该方法相比精确计算卷积神经切线核（CNTK）实现了106倍的加速。
AI.conclusion: 本文方法突破了传统核方法对ReLU激活的依赖，显著提升了神经网络核计算的效率，为大规模模型优化提供了新思路。

Moreover, most prior works on neural kernels have focused on the ReLU activation, mainly due to its popularity but also due to the difficulty of computing such kernels for general activations. In this work, we overcome such difficulties by providing methods to work with general activations. First, we compile and expand the list of activation functions admitting exact dual activation expressions to compute neural kernels. When the exact computation is unknown, we present methods to effectively approximate them. We propose a fast sketching method that approximates any multi-layered Neural Network Gaussian Process (NNGP) kernel and Neural Tangent Kernel (NTK) matrices for a wide range of activation functions, going beyond the commonly analyzed ReLU activation. This is done by showing how to approximate the neural kernels using the truncated Hermite expansion of any desired activation functions. While most prior works require data points on the unit sphere, our methods do not suffer from such limitations and are applicable to any dataset of points in $\mathbb{R}^d$. Furthermore, we provide a subspace embedding for NNGP and NTK matrices with near input-sparsity runtime and near-optimal target dimension which applies to any \emph{homogeneous} dual activation functions with rapidly convergent Taylor expansion. Empirically, with respect to exact convolutional NTK (CNTK) computation, our method achieves $106\times$ speedup for approximate CNTK of a 5-layer Myrtle network on CIFAR-10 dataset. 无限宽度限制通过在神经网络和核方法之间建立联系，阐明了深度学习的泛化和优化方面。尽管它们很重要，但由于它们的（超）二次运行时间和内存复杂性，这些内核方法的实用性在大规模学习环境中受到限制。此外，大多数先前关于神经内核的工作都集中在 ReLU 激活上，这主要是因为它的流行，但也因为计算此类内核用于一般激活的困难。在这项工作中，我们通过提供处理一般激活的方法来克服这些困难。首先，我们编译并扩展激活函数列表，允许精确的双激活表达式来计算神经内核。当确切的计算未知时，我们提出了有效近似它们的方法。我们提出了一种快速草图方法，可以近似任何多层神经网络高斯过程 (NNGP) 内核和神经正切内核 (NTK) 矩阵，以实现各种激活函数，超越了通常分析的 ReLU 激活。这是通过展示如何使用任何所需激活函​​数的截断埃尔米特展开来近似神经核来完成的。虽然大多数先前的工作需要单位球体上的数据点，但我们的方法不受此类限制，并且适用于 $\mathbb{R}^d$ 中的任何点数据集。此外，我们为 NNGP 和 NTK 矩阵提供了一个具有接近输入稀疏运行时间和接近最优目标维度的子空间嵌入，适用于任何具有快速收敛泰勒展开的 \emph{齐次} 对偶激活函数。根据经验，对于精确卷积 NTK (CNTK) 计算，我们的方法在 CIFAR-10 数据集上对 5 层 Myrtle 网络的近似 CNTK 实现了 106 倍加速。 本文提出了一种高效计算神经网络核嵌入的方法，适用于通用激活函数，解决了传统方法在大规模学习中的计算复杂度高和激活函数限制的问题。 Insu Han,Amir Zandieh,Jaehoon Lee,Roman Novak,Lechao Xiao,Amin Karbasi cs.LG,cs.AI,stat.ML

title: An Artificial Chemistry Implementation of a Gene Regulatory Network
title_zh: 基因调控网络的人工化学实现
comment: 20, pages, 12 figures
comments: 20, pages, 12 figures
abstract: Gene Regulatory Networks are networks of interactions in biological organisms responsible for determining the production levels of proteins and peptides. Proteins are workers of a cell factory, and their production defines the goal of a cell and its development. Various attempts have been made to model such networks both to understand these biological systems better and to use inspiration from understanding them to solve computational problems. In this work, a biologically more realistic model for gene regulatory networks is proposed, which incorporates Cellular Automata and Artificial Chemistry to model the interactions between regulatory proteins called the Transcription Factors and the regulatory sites of genes. The result of this work shows complex dynamics close to what can be observed in nature. Here, an analysis of the impact of the initial states of the system on the produced dynamics is performed, showing that such evolvable models can be directed towards producing desired protein dynamics.

The result of this work shows complex dynamics close to what can be observed in nature. Here, an analysis of the impact of the initial states of the system on the produced dynamics is performed, showing that such evolvable models can be directed towards producing desired protein dynamics.
abstract_zh: 基因调控网络是生物有机体中负责确定蛋白质和肽的生产水平的相互作用网络。蛋白质是细胞工厂的工人，它们的生产决定了细胞及其发育的目标。人们已经做出了各种尝试来对此类网络进行建模，以便更好地理解这些生物系统，并利用理解它们的灵感来解决计算问题。在这项工作中，提出了一种在生物学上更现实的基因调控网络模型，该模型结合了细胞自动机和人工化学来模拟称为转录因子的调控蛋白与基因调控位点之间的相互作用。这项工作的结果显示了接近自然界中观察到的复杂动态。在这里，对系统初始状态对产生的动力学的影响进行了分析，表明这种可进化的模型可以直接产生所需的蛋白质动力学。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合细胞自动机和人工化学的基因调控网络模型，以模拟生物体内蛋白质生产的复杂动态。
AI.motivation: 为更准确地理解生物系统并利用其原理解决计算问题，需构建更贴近生物学实际的基因调控网络模型。
AI.method: 通过整合细胞自动机与人工化学方法，模拟转录因子与基因调控位点之间的相互作用，构建动态模型。
AI.result: 模型展现出类似自然界的复杂动态，并分析了初始状态对蛋白质动态生成的影响，证明模型可被引导以实现特定目标。
AI.conclusion: 该模型有效模拟了基因调控网络的生物学特性，通过调整初始条件可定向生成所需蛋白质动态，为生物研究与计算应用提供新思路。

title: Robust and Lossless Fingerprinting of Deep Neural Networks via Pooled Membership Inference
title_zh: 通过池成员推理对深度神经网络进行稳健且无损的指纹识别
comment: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=IdiF7M0AAAAJ&hl=en
comments: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=IdiF7M0AAAAJ&hl=en
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have already achieved great success in a lot of application areas and brought profound changes to our society. However, it also raises new security problems, among which how to protect the intellectual property (IP) of DNNs against infringement is one of the most important yet very challenging topics. To deal with this problem, recent studies focus on the IP protection of DNNs by applying digital watermarking, which embeds source information and/or authentication data into DNN models by tuning network parameters directly or indirectly. However, tuning network parameters inevitably distorts the DNN and therefore surely impairs the performance of the DNN model on its original task regardless of the degree of the performance degradation. It has motivated the authors in this paper to propose a novel technique called pooled membership inference (PMI) so as to protect the IP of the DNN models. The proposed PMI neither alters the network parameters of the given DNN model nor fine-tunes the DNN model with a sequence of carefully crafted trigger samples. Instead, it leaves the original DNN model unchanged, but can determine the ownership of the DNN model by inferring which mini-dataset among multiple mini-datasets was once used to train the target DNN model, which differs from previous arts and has remarkable potential in practice. Experiments also have demonstrated the superiority and applicability of this work.
summary: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have already achieved great success in a lot of application areas and brought profound changes to our society. However, it also raises new security problems, among which how to protect the intellectual property (IP) of DNNs against infringement is one of the most important yet very challenging topics. To deal with this problem, recent studies focus on the IP protection of DNNs by applying digital watermarking, which embeds source information and/or authentication data into DNN models by tuning network parameters directly or indirectly. However, tuning network parameters inevitably distorts the DNN and therefore surely impairs the performance of the DNN model on its original task regardless of the degree of the performance degradation. It has motivated the authors in this paper to propose a novel technique called pooled membership inference (PMI) so as to protect the IP of the DNN models. The proposed PMI neither alters the network parameters of the given DNN model nor fine-tunes the DNN model with a sequence of carefully crafted trigger samples. Instead, it leaves the original DNN model unchanged, but can determine the ownership of the DNN model by inferring which mini-dataset among multiple mini-datasets was once used to train the target DNN model, which differs from previous arts and has remarkable potential in practice. Experiments also have demonstrated the superiority and applicability of this work.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络（DNN）已经在许多应用领域取得了巨大成功，给我们的社会带来了深刻的变化。然而，它也带来了新的安全问题，其中如何保护DNN的知识产权（IP）免受侵权是最重要但最具挑战性的课题之一。为了解决这个问题，最近的研究重点是通过应用数字水印来保护 DNN 的 IP，通过直接或间接调整网络参数将源信息和/或身份验证数据嵌入到 DNN 模型中。然而，调整网络参数不可避免地会扭曲 DNN，因此无论性能下降的程度如何，都肯定会损害 DNN 模型在其原始任务上的性能。这促使本文作者提出一种称为池成员推理 (PMI) 的新技术，以保护 DNN 模型的 IP。所提出的 PMI 既不会改变给定 DNN 模型的网络参数，也不会使用一系列精心设计的触发样本来微调 DNN 模型。相反，它保持原始DNN模型不变，而是通过推断多个迷你数据集中的哪个迷你数据集曾经用于训练目标DNN模型来确定DNN模型的归属，这与以往的技术不同，在实践中具有显着的潜力。实验也证明了本文工作的优越性和适用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的深度神经网络（DNN）指纹技术，称为池化成员推理（PMI），用于保护DNN的知识产权。
AI.motivation: 现有的数字水印方法通过调整网络参数来保护DNN的知识产权，但会损害模型性能。因此需要一种不改变网络参数的方法。
AI.method: PMI方法通过推理目标DNN模型是否使用了特定的微数据集进行训练，从而确定其所有权，而无需改变模型参数或使用触发样本。
AI.result: 实验表明，PMI方法在保护DNN知识产权方面具有优越性和适用性。
AI.conclusion: PMI方法为DNN的知识产权保护提供了一种新的、不损害模型性能的解决方案。

authors_text: Shunjie Chen,Xiaochuan Shi,Jingye Li,Shengqiong Wu,Hao Fei,Fei Li,Donghong Ji
title: Joint Alignment of Multi-Task Feature and Label Spaces for Emotion Cause Pair Extraction
title_zh: 多任务特征和标签空间的联合对齐用于情感原因对提取
comment: Accepted by Coling 2022
comments: Accepted by Coling 2022
abstract: Emotion cause pair extraction (ECPE), as one of the derived subtasks of emotion cause analysis (ECA), shares rich inter-related features with emotion extraction (EE) and cause extraction (CE). Therefore EE and CE are frequently utilized as auxiliary tasks for better feature learning, modeled via multi-task learning (MTL) framework by prior works to achieve state-of-the-art (SoTA) ECPE results. However, existing MTL-based methods either fail to simultaneously model the specific features and the interactive feature in between, or suffer from the inconsistency of label prediction. In this work, we consider addressing the above challenges for improving ECPE by performing two alignment mechanisms with a novel A^2Net model. We first propose a feature-task alignment to explicitly model the specific emotion-&cause-specific features and the shared interactive feature. Besides, an inter-task alignment is implemented, in which the label distance between the ECPE and the combinations of EE&CE are learned to be narrowed for better label consistency. Evaluations of benchmarks show that our methods outperform current best-performing systems on all ECA subtasks. Further analysis proves the importance of our proposed alignment mechanisms for the task.
summary: Emotion cause pair extraction (ECPE), as one of the derived subtasks of emotion cause analysis (ECA), shares rich inter-related features with emotion extraction (EE) and cause extraction (CE). Therefore EE and CE are frequently utilized as auxiliary tasks for better feature learning, modeled via multi-task learning (MTL) framework by prior works to achieve state-of-the-art (SoTA) ECPE results. However, existing MTL-based methods either fail to simultaneously model the specific features and the interactive feature in between, or suffer from the inconsistency of label prediction. In this work, we consider addressing the above challenges for improving ECPE by performing two alignment mechanisms with a novel A^2Net model. We first propose a feature-task alignment to explicitly model the specific emotion-&cause-specific features and the shared interactive feature. Besides, an inter-task alignment is implemented, in which the label distance between the ECPE and the combinations of EE&CE are learned to be narrowed for better label consistency. Evaluations of benchmarks show that our methods outperform current best-performing systems on all ECA subtasks. Further analysis proves the importance of our proposed alignment mechanisms for the task.
abstract_zh: 情感原因对提取（ECPE）作为情感原因分析（ECA）的派生子任务之一，与情感提取（EE）和原因提取（CE）共享丰富的相互关联的特征。因此，EE 和 CE 经常被用作辅助任务，以实现更好的特征学习，通过先前的工作通过多任务学习（MTL）框架进行建模，以实现最先进的（SoTA）ECPE 结果。然而，现有的基于 MTL 的方法要么无法同时对特定特征和其间的交互特征进行建模，要么面临标签预测不一致的问题。在这项工作中，我们考虑通过使用新颖的 A^2Net 模型执行两种对齐机制来解决上述改进 ECPE 的挑战。我们首先提出了一种特征-任务对齐来显式地建模特定的情感和原因特定特征以及共享的交互特征。此外，还实现了任务间对齐，其中 ECPE 与 EE&CE 组合之间的标签距离被学习以缩小以获得更好的标签一致性。基准评估表明，我们的方法在所有 ECA 子任务上均优于当前表现最佳的系统。进一步的分析证明了我们提出的任务协调机制的重要性。

AI.motivation: 现有基于多任务学习（MTL）的方法无法同时建模特定特征与交互特征，且标签预测存在不一致性，影响ECPE效果。

AI.result: 在基准测试中，该方法在所有ECA子任务上均优于现有最佳系统，验证了对齐机制的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 联合对齐机制能有效提升多任务特征学习与标签一致性，为ECPE提供新思路。

search_text: Joint Alignment of Multi-Task Feature and Label Spaces for Emotion Cause Pair Extraction 多任务特征和标签空间的联合对齐用于情感原因对提取 Emotion cause pair extraction (ECPE), as one of the derived subtasks of emotion cause analysis (ECA), shares rich inter-related features with emotion extraction (EE) and cause extraction (CE). Therefore EE and CE are frequently utilized as auxiliary tasks for better feature learning, modeled via multi-task learning (MTL) framework by prior works to achieve state-of-the-art (SoTA) ECPE results. However, existing MTL-based methods either fail to simultaneously model the specific features and the interactive feature in between, or suffer from the inconsistency of label prediction. In this work, we consider addressing the above challenges for improving ECPE by performing two alignment mechanisms with a novel A^2Net model. We first propose a feature-task alignment to explicitly model the specific emotion-&cause-specific features and the shared interactive feature. Besides, an inter-task alignment is implemented, in which the label distance between the ECPE and the combinations of EE&CE are learned to be narrowed for better label consistency. Evaluations of benchmarks show that our methods outperform current best-performing systems on all ECA subtasks. Further analysis proves the importance of our proposed alignment mechanisms for the task. 情感原因对提取（ECPE）作为情感原因分析（ECA）的派生子任务之一，与情感提取（EE）和原因提取（CE）共享丰富的相互关联的特征。因此，EE 和 CE 经常被用作辅助任务，以实现更好的特征学习，通过先前的工作通过多任务学习（MTL）框架进行建模，以实现最先进的（SoTA）ECPE 结果。然而，现有的基于 MTL 的方法要么无法同时对特定特征和其间的交互特征进行建模，要么面临标签预测不一致的问题。在这项工作中，我们考虑通过使用新颖的 A^2Net 模型执行两种对齐机制来解决上述改进 ECPE 的挑战。我们首先提出了一种特征-任务对齐来显式地建模特定的情感和原因特定特征以及共享的交互特征。此外，还实现了任务间对齐，其中 ECPE 与 EE&CE 组合之间的标签距离被学习以缩小以获得更好的标签一致性。基准评估表明，我们的方法在所有 ECA 子任务上均优于当前表现最佳的系统。进一步的分析证明了我们提出的任务协调机制的重要性。 本文提出A²Net模型，通过联合对齐多任务特征和标签空间，改进情绪原因对提取（ECPE）任务。 Shunjie Chen,Xiaochuan Shi,Jingye Li,Shengqiong Wu,Hao Fei,Fei Li,Donghong Ji cs.CL,cs.AI
